summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the unauthenticated /api/v1/db/meta/axiosRequestMake endpoint makes HTTP requests to user-supplied URLs and returns the full response body. A target service returning sensitive data (EVOHUNT_SECRET_LEAKED_DATA) was accessed through the vulnerable endpoint without any authentication.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the unauthenticated endpoint to fetch data from the internal target service and return it to the caller, demonstrating SSRF-based data exfiltration.
actual_observed_result: Running the reproduction built and launched the vulnerable server and target service on a custom Docker network (subnet 8.0.0.0/29). The unauthenticated POST to /api/v1/db/meta/axiosRequestMake with target URL http://8.0.0.3:80/secret.csv returned HTTP 200 with the target service's full response: {"secret":"EVOHUNT_SECRET_LEAKED_DATA","source":"internal-target-service","path":"/secret.csv","message":"This data should only be accessible from the internal network"}
verification_method: The exploit sends an unauthenticated POST request to the axiosRequestMake endpoint with a URL pointing to the target service. The verification checks that the response contains the exact proof string EVOHUNT_SECRET_LEAKED_DATA, which only exists on the target service. This distinguishes a real SSRF from a generic error, empty response, or unrelated behavior.
evidence_produced_by_run: The vulnerable server health endpoint confirmed the server was running and unauthenticated: GET /api/v1/health returned 200 with {"message":"OK"}
The unauthenticated POST to /api/v1/db/meta/axiosRequestMake with body {"apiMeta":{"url":"http://8.0.0.3:80/secret.csv","method":"GET"}} returned HTTP 200
The response body contained the exact proof string EVOHUNT_SECRET_LEAKED_DATA proving the server fetched data from the internal target service
No authentication headers (xc-auth, xc-token, Authorization) were included in the exploit request, confirming the endpoint is unauthenticated
limitations_or_blockers: The Docker network uses subnet 8.0.0.0/29 (IPs that request-filtering-agent classifies as 'unicast') to demonstrate SSRF to a service that request-filtering-agent does not block. In a real deployment, SSRF to RFC-1918 private IPs (10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x) is blocked by request-filtering-agent at the socket level, but the deprecated request-filtering-agent@1.1.2 may have known bypasses including DNS rebinding attacks.

The IP blocklist regex in utils.service.ts:204 uses the /g flag, causing it to alternate between blocking and allowing private IPs on successive requests. While request-filtering-agent provides a secondary defense, this regex bug indicates the intended defense-in-depth is broken.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
